label: AI news / announcement
opening_rule: First sentence: your assessment of whether this announcement actually matters -- not what was announced, not who made it. Max 15 words. Must work as a standalone tweet.
closing_rule: Never start the final paragraph with 'In conclusion', 'The definitive assessment', 'Ultimately', 'To summarize', or any phrase that signals a summary. End on a specific observation -- a detail, a consequence, or an irony that follows from the argument. If the technology changes how something fundamental works, use one concrete analogy to make it tangible -- not a cliche.

sections.name: What happened
sections.purpose: The news itself -- factual, concise, no spin. Who, what, when, how. No interpretation yet.

sections.name: What it means
sections.purpose: Practical impact for developers, companies, or end users. Name specific consequences -- not vague 'implications'. If the impact is unclear, say so directly.

sections.name: Industry context
sections.purpose: How this fits the current AI landscape and competitive dynamics. Name the players affected and how. Who gains, who loses, who is irrelevant.

sections.name: Why it matters or doesn't
sections.purpose: Your verdict on whether this announcement changes anything real. Is the hype justified? Who actually benefits and who is being sold a dream? Take a position -- do not list open questions. If the technology changes how something works at a fundamental level, use one concrete analogy to make it tangible -- not a cliche. Do not write a summary sentence. End on a specific observation, not a verdict formula.
